name: theanine
summary: Non-protein amino acid from tea associated with calming and attention-related effects.
description: Non-protein amino acid from tea associated with calming and attention-related effects.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.

claimMap.claim: 400 mg/day L-theanine for 28 days has been studied in healthy adults with moderate stress, with signals for perceived stress and attention but small sample limitations.
claimMap.notes: 400 mg/day L-theanine for 28 days has been studied in healthy adults with moderate stress, with signals for perceived stress and attention but small sample limitations.

qualifiers.population: Healthy adults age 18–65 with moderate stress

claimMap.claim: L-theanine was tolerated in a small 28-day trial, but the sample was small and not enough for broad safety certainty.
claimMap.notes: L-theanine was tolerated in a small 28-day trial, but the sample was small and not enough for broad safety certainty.

claimMap.claim: Standalone L-theanine has been studied across 50–900 mg/day; 200–450 mg/day appears to be the most useful adult sleep-support range in current review evidence.
claimMap.notes: Standalone L-theanine has been studied across 50–900 mg/day; 200–450 mg/day appears to be the most useful adult sleep-support range in current review evidence.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: Standalone L-theanine 50–900 mg/day studied; review suggests 200–450 mg/day may support healthy sleep in adults
qualifiers.population: Humans; adult-focused interpretation for sleep support

claimMap.claim: Theanine, especially with caffeine, may support some attention and mood outcomes, but confidence intervals often show uncertainty.
claimMap.notes: Theanine, especially with caffeine, may support some attention and mood outcomes, but confidence intervals often show uncertainty.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: Theanine alone or with caffeine; acute outcomes

claimMap.claim: A single 200 mg dose of AlphaWave L-theanine has been studied for acute stress response/relaxation markers in healthy moderately stressed adults.
claimMap.notes: A single 200 mg dose of AlphaWave L-theanine has been studied for acute stress response/relaxation markers in healthy moderately stressed adults.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: Single dose of AlphaWave L-Theanine 200 mg; effects assessed up to 3 hours post-dose

claimMap.claim: A small 28-day trial found safety/tolerability and some perceived stress, sleep-quality, and attention signals.
claimMap.notes: A small 28-day trial found safety/tolerability and some perceived stress, sleep-quality, and attention signals.

qualifiers.population: 30 healthy adults with moderate stress, age 18-65

claimMap.claim: L-theanine may be studied as an adjunct in mental health contexts, but pages should not imply it treats psychiatric disorders or replaces medication.
claimMap.notes: L-theanine may be studied as an adjunct in mental health contexts, but pages should not imply it treats psychiatric disorders or replaces medication.

qualifiers.population: People with schizophrenia, ADHD, OCD, MDD, sleep disorders, GAD, Tourette syndrome

claimMap.claim: L-theanine may support subjective sleep quality and sleep-onset related outcomes, but more pure L-theanine trials are needed.
claimMap.notes: L-theanine may support subjective sleep quality and sleep-onset related outcomes, but more pure L-theanine trials are needed.

qualifiers.direction: positive but more pure-L-theanine trials needed
qualifiers.dose_or_duration: 50-900 mg/day review range; 200-450 mg/day appears common for sleep-support contexts

sources.title: The effects of L-theanine consumption on sleep outcomes
The effects of L-theanine supplementation on outcomes of patients with mental disorders
sources.citation: 11 RCTs; psychiatric populations; further studies needed to validate effects
sources.note: claim framing for mental health adjunct contexts

sources.title: Safety and Efficacy of AlphaWave L-Theanine Supplementation for 28 Days
Single dose AlphaWave L-Theanine on stress
sources.citation: Single 200 mg AlphaWave L-Theanine; stress challenge; 1–3 hour markers

sources.title: Examining the effect of L-theanine on sleep
sources.citation: 13 trials; 550 participants; standalone 50–900 mg/day; 200–450 mg/day useful adult sleep-support range

sources.title: Effects of Tea or its Bioactive Compounds l-Theanine or l-Theanine plus Caffeine on Cognition, Sleep, and Mood
